more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: Green Ambitions, Hard Realities: Reimagining Innovation and Sustainable Business Building in Zimbabwe
more.description: Zimbabwe stands at an unusual intersection of necessity and opportunity. On one hand, the pressures are unmistakable: energy insecurity, climate variability, industrial stagnation, and constrained capital. On the other hand, the country possesses the raw ingredients of a green transition: abundant sunlight, agricultural depth, mineral wealth, and a youthful population increasingly attuned to technology and […]The post Green Ambitions, Hard Realities: Reimagining Innovation and Sustainable Business Building in Zimbabwe appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

more.title: Zimbabwe emerges as SADC’s second largest economy
more.description: Zimbabwe’s economy has been officially rebased to an estimated US$52,4 billion for 2025, positioning the country as the second-largest economy in Southern Africa after South Africa. The revised figures, released by Zimbabwe National Statistics Agency, follow a comprehensive economic census conducted in 2023 that captured previously underreported sectors, particularly the informal economy. more.title: Firearm Found at Plumtree Border Post: How Illegal Guns are Flooding Zimbabwe as ED2030 Agenda Turns Dangerous
more.description: PLUMTREE – On a sweltering afternoon at the Plumtree Border Post, a routine customs inspection by Zimbabwe Revenue Authority (ZIMRA) officials took a turn from the mundane to the chilling. Amidst the usual clutter of cross-border trade—second-hand clothes, basic groceries, and electronic parts—officers stumbled upon a concealed, undeclared firearm hidden deep within a traveller’s suitcase. more.description: Zimbabwe’s manufacturing sector stands at a defining crossroads.
